title: Fix link inconsistencies in man pages
body:
Found via:

    echo for i in docs/*.md;do x=$(perl -ne 'if (/\[(podman-.*?)\(1\)\]\((podman-.*?)\.1\.md/) { print "  $1 != $2\n" if $1 ne $2; print " ENOENT $2\n" unless -e "./docs/$2.1.md"}' <$i); if [ -n "$x" ]; then echo $i; echo "$x";fi;done

...which is probably a good candidate for another CI hook,
except I have no idea how to rewrite it in awk.

There's one remaining inconsistency: podman-refresh in podman.1.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: We need to deprecate refresh. It used to have a purpose, but no longer...
@mheon should I preemptively just remove it from `podman.1.md` as part of this PR, then leave it as TBD for someone else to remove from the command list and `podman-container.1.md` ?

So done, thanks. This breaks the in-development `hack/podman-commands.sh` script, but that's probably a good thing.

There's another problem with my PR: removing `podman-help` from `podman.1.md` also breaks the compare script. It also doesn't show "podman help" as a command in the man page, but since it does document `--help`, and since it truly offends me to link to a nonexistent `podman-help(1)`, I think the PR in its current state is the least ugly solution. But as always, there may be something I've missed.
@edsantiago Remove refresh and make it hidden.

New force-push: marks `refresh` hidden, and removes it from `podman(1)` and `podman-container(1)`, but leaves `podman-container-refresh(1)` untouched. Please LMK if you think I should remove the self-referential `podman` link in `podman(1)`.
Remove the podman-container-refresh man page as well.

New force push. Removed `docs/podman-container-refresh.1.md` as requested. AFAICT this does not need changes to the Makefile -- the doc list is computed via wildcarding. Same with specfile. I may of course have missed something else.

@rhatdan if I understand your comment in IRC just now, you're asking me to make this change?
```
 | info     | [podman-system-info(1)](podman-info.1.md) |
```
If so, may I humbly push back on that? My reason for wanting it as it is, is for the consistency check (perl oneliner in commit message) to pass cleanly. Although that script is not a CI hook right now, I'd like to spend time next week translating that into something that could be used as a hook.

If I understand your concern, it's that my current way displays inconsistently in the man page: prune and renumber point to `podman-system-xxx` man pages, but info to `podman-info`. Is that your concern? If so, my argument is that the man page reference should be to the canonical command; which in this case is `podman-info`. There is ample precedent for this convention, see `podman-image(1)` and `podman-container(1)`, both of which overwhelmingly refer to `podman-XXX` (canonical names) instead of `podman-subcommand-xxx`.

That said, I will go with whatever you reply.

Ok you are right, we treated it that way on the others, although all the man pages exists(at least as links.)
/lgtm
